div1.entryFree: Lifeless or rather Liveless (for such is the spelling of all O. Edd.) destitute of life, unanimated, dead: Ven. 211. Lucr. 1374. As I, 2, 263. H5 IV, 2, 55. H6B IV, 1, 142. hopeless and helpless doth Aegeon wend, but to procrastinate his l. end, Err. I, 1, 159, perhaps not the end brought on by death, but the end of his lifeless state, the end of his death-like life.
